scenario_title: Name_absent prereq with delete_rrset to absent type
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified corner where the prerequisite explicitly establishes that l.campus.edu. is absent, then the UPDATE requests delete_rrset for A at that same name. Because the name is already absent, some servers may accept the request as vacuously satisfied and return NOERROR, while others may reject it as a semantic contradiction and return REFUSED or another error. The follow-up QUERY is included to confirm the resulting state and keep the oracle consistent, but the primary observable difference is the UPDATE reply.

group_1.behavior: Treat the delete_rrset at a prerequisite-absent name as a valid no-op after the name_absent prerequisite has been satisfied.
group_1.post_state: l.campus.edu. remains absent; QUERY returns NXDOMAIN or otherwise no A rrset.

group_2.behavior: Reject the UPDATE as semantically inconsistent even though the name_absent prerequisite passed.
group_2.post_state: No change; QUERY still shows l.campus.edu. absent with no A rrset.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.1 defines the name_absent prerequisite, and §3.4.2.2 defines delete_rrset, but the RFC does not say whether deleting an rrset at a name just proven absent is a harmless no-op or an error. That leaves the UPDATE reply code implementation-defined even when the message is otherwise valid.
